steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the dimensions
steps.content: From the problem: width = $w$ feet and length = $1.59w$ feet.

steps.title: Apply the area formula
steps.content: For a rectangle, Area = length × width. Substituting: $$A = (1.59w)(w)$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $A = (1.59w)(w)$: Multiplies length $1.59w$ by width $w$ directly from the area formula. ✓
  • Choice B — $A = \left(\frac{1.59w}{w}\right)(w)$: The fraction $\frac{1.59w}{w}$ simplifies to $1.59$, giving $A = 1.59w$ (just the length, not area). ✗
  • Choice C — $A = (w - 1.59)(w)$: Subtracts $1.59$ from $w$ instead of multiplying; has no basis in the given length. ✗
  • Choice D — $A = (w + 1.59)(w)$: Adds $1.59$ to $w$, confusing the coefficient with an addend. ✗
A is correct.
